data.title: Graham Corporation buys FlackTek, boosting engineered products; acquisitions expand materials processing capabilities.
data.description: Batavia, New York - Graham Corporation (NYSE: GHM), a prominent company specializing in critical fluid, power, heat transfer, and vacuum technologies for the Defense, Energy & Process, and Space industries, has revealed the acquisition of FlackTek Manufacturing, LLC and FlackTek Sales, LLC ("FlackTek"). FlackTek, known for its innovative mixing and material processing solutions, strengthens Graham Corporation's portfolio. This addition marks advanced materials processing as a new core focus for GHM.

data.title: Onity Group offers $150 million of Senior Notes due 2029 through its subsidiaries PHH Corporation and PHH Escrow Issuer LLC.
data.description: On January 26, 2026, in West Palm Beach, Florida, Onity Group Inc. revealed that its subsidiaries PHH Corporation and PHH Escrow Issuer LLC have initiated an offer of $150 million in total principal amount of 9.875% Senior Notes due 2029 (referred to as the “PHH Senior Notes”). This offering is contingent on various factors including market conditions. Onity Group Inc. trades on the NYSE under the symbol ONIT.

data.title: Pulsenmore and Clalit Health collaborate for at-home follicular monitoring service for IVF, valued at $4.5 million over 5 years.
data.description: A commercial deal worth around $4.5 million is on the horizon for Pulsenmore Ltd. Following an 18-month pilot, this agreement is projected to span five years. The service is set to be rolled out through Beilinson NEXT, Beilinson's virtual hospital located in Ramat Gan, Israel. Pulsenmore Ltd., a global company listed on NASDAQ as PLSM and TASE as PLSM, announced the details on Jan. 26, 2026.

data.description: Founder pays tribute to the company's heritage by launching an innovative "Pop Pack" campaign for the Big Game, aimed at energizing and involving supporters. data.title: Forgent Power Solutions launching IPO roadshow for 39,413,177 shares of Class A common stock by Neos Partners-controlled entities.
data.description: Forgent Power Solutions, Inc., a prominent manufacturer of electrical distribution equipment for data centers and industrial facilities, has commenced its roadshow for the initial public offering of its Class A common stock. The offering includes 39,413,177 shares of Class A common stock from parent entities controlled by Neos Partners.

data.title: G2 Consulting Group buys Construction Testing Services, enhancing presence in Bay Area and Thumb Region. Acquisition marks company's expansion.
data.description: In Troy, Michigan, a prominent company, G2 Consulting Group, widely recognized for its geotechnical, environmental, and construction engineering services, recently disclosed its acquisition of Construction Testing Services. The latter, a well-regarded engineering services firm boasting over four decades of experience, was seen as a strategic move by G2 Consulting Group to strengthen its position in the industry. The deal, finalized on January 26, 2026, underscores G2 Consulting Group’s commitment to growth and expanding its offerings.

data.title: New CIO, Rohan Pal, appointed at NCR Atleos to drive technology strategy and digital transformation.
data.description: Atleos, a prominent player in enhancing self-service financial access, disclosed the selection of Rohan Pal as their Chief Information Officer (CIO), underscoring their dedication to innovation, operational proficiency, and expansion. The appointment of Pal, in a deliberate and meticulous recruitment endeavor, signifies the importance of securing a CIO with the appropriate skills, alignment with core values, and belief in the company's vision. Atleos Corporation, listed on NYSE as NATL, aims to advance further with this strategic leadership decision.

data.title: Increased need for doctors in healthcare marketing for trust and credibility due to rising misinformation.
data.description: In today's landscape rampant with misinformation, establishing trust and credibility in the market is essential. Recent reports indicate that companies in the Healthcare & Wellness sector, based in Chattanooga, Tennessee, are encountering heightened levels of scrutiny. With the date set at January 26, 2026, concerns range from ensuring the accuracy of medical claims and data integrity to combating the swift dissemination of misleading information. The need to solidify trust has become increasingly pressing in this industry.

data.title: Bitcoin below $88,000, market sentiment led to $744.06 million liquidations. Bitcoin ETFs had $103.6 million outflows and Ethereum ETFs had $41.7 million outflows. Other crypto prices: Ethereum $2,905, Solana $122.39, XRP $1.88, Dogecoin $0.1206.
data.description: Bitcoin is kicking off the week hovering around the $88,000 mark amid a fearful market sentiment that led to liquidations totaling $744.06 million within the last 24 hours. Bitcoin ETFs experienced $103.6 million in net outflows on Friday, whereas Ethereum ETFs witnessed $41.7 million in net outflows. Current cryptocurrency prices include Bitcoin at $87,856.02, Ethereum at $2,905, Solana at $122.39, XRP at $1.88, Dogecoin at $0.1206, and Shiba Inu at $0.057636. Noteworthy trader Timeless Being mentioned that Bitcoin is currently testing a critical level crucial for the bulls within the existing range. More information can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: CVR Partners reports preliminary estimated 2025 results; delay due to third-party downtime.
data.description: CVR Partners, LP (NYSE: UAN) of Sugar Land, Texas revealed initial estimated financial figures for Q4 and full-year 2025. The Coffeyville facility's scheduled overhaul was finished in early November, but the relaunch was postponed by a few weeks due to issues with the air separation unit owned by a third party. Mark Pytosh, the CEO, mentioned, "Despite the setback, we observed strong...".

data.title: CVR Energy reports Q4 & full-year 2025 results, including net loss due to depreciation.
data.description: CVR Energy, Inc. unveiled its preliminary estimated financial results for Q4 and the full year of 2025, with a focus on the negative impact on the company's performance. The company, listed as CVI on the NYSE, reported a significant net loss attributed to CVR Energy stockholders in Q4 2025. This was largely influenced by the accelerated depreciation linked to the transition of the renewable diesel unit at Wynnewood to hydrocarbon processing. Additionally, a decrease in nitrogen fertilizer production also contributed to the challenging financial outcome.

data.title: MKS plans a €1.0 billion private offering of senior notes due 2034, subject to market conditions.
data.description: In Andover, Massachusetts on January 26, 2026, MKS Inc. (NASDAQ: MKSI) disclosed its plan to conduct a private offering. The offering consists of €1.0 billion aggregate principal amount of senior notes due 2034. This is subject to various conditions, including market circumstances, with no guarantee on the completion, timing, or terms of the offering.

data.title: Once Upon a Farm begins its initial public offering, offering 10,997,209 shares of common stock, with 7,631,537 by them.
data.description: Today, Once Upon a Farm has initiated the roadshow for its upcoming initial public offering. The company filed a registration statement on Form S-1 with the SEC to offer a total of 10,997,209 shares of its common stock. Out of these, 7,631,537 shares will be offered by Once Upon a Farm, while 3,365,672 shares will be offered by existing stockholders. Once Upon a Farm is also planning to go public with this offering.
